label: Strong bid with specific case studies, named staff, clear methodology
text:
Approach to Delivering the Data Analytics Platform

Our methodology for delivering the council's data analytics platform is grounded in seven years of public sector data infrastructure delivery. We propose a three-phase approach spanning 18 weeks, with fortnightly sprint reviews and a named Product Owner embedded in the client team.

Phase 1 (Weeks 1-4): Discovery and Data Audit. Our Lead Data Architect, Dr Priya Sharma (15 years' experience, previously Head of Data Engineering at NHS Digital), will conduct a comprehensive audit of existing data sources, quality issues, and integration requirements. She led an identical exercise for Birmingham City Council in 2024, mapping 147 data sources across 12 departments and producing a data quality scorecard that reduced duplicate records by 34% within three months.

Phase 2 (Weeks 5-12): Platform Build and Integration. Our engineering team of four, led by Senior Developer Marcus Chen, will build the analytics platform using a modern lakehouse architecture on Azure. In our most recent comparable project for Transport for Greater Manchester, we integrated 23 real-time data feeds and delivered a dashboard suite that reduced report generation time from 3 days to 15 minutes, saving an estimated 1,200 staff hours annually.

Phase 3 (Weeks 13-18): Testing, Training, and Handover. We will deliver 40 hours of structured training to up to 30 staff members, graded by technical proficiency. Our training materials will be tailored to the council's specific data and use cases. Post-go-live, our Support Lead, Amina Osei, will provide 12 weeks of dedicated support with a guaranteed 4-hour response time for critical issues.

Risk Mitigation: We maintain a named backup for each key role. Data migration risks will be managed through our proven parallel-run methodology, which we have used successfully on 11 previous public sector projects with zero data loss incidents.

label: Boilerplate bid with generic claims and no evidence
text:
Our Approach to Service Delivery

We have extensive experience in delivering projects of this nature and are confident in our ability to meet all of the council's requirements. Our team of highly qualified professionals brings a wealth of knowledge and expertise to every engagement. We understand the challenges facing local authorities and are committed to supporting you in achieving your objectives.

We pride ourselves on our collaborative approach, working closely with clients to understand their unique needs and deliver tailored solutions that make a real difference. Our track record speaks for itself, with numerous successful projects delivered across the public sector. Client satisfaction is at the heart of everything we do.

We will assign a dedicated project manager who will serve as your single point of contact throughout the engagement. Our proven methodology ensures that projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to the highest standards of quality. We employ rigorous quality assurance processes at every stage of delivery to ensure consistency and excellence.

We look forward to the opportunity to demonstrate our capabilities and build a long-term partnership with the council. Our team is ready to mobilise immediately and hit the ground running from day one.

label: Average bid with one good case study, rest generic
text:
Methodology for the Evaluation and Research Programme

We propose a mixed-methods approach combining quantitative impact analysis with qualitative stakeholder engagement to deliver a robust evaluation framework for the programme.

Our team has relevant experience in this area. In 2024, we delivered the evaluation of the Greater Leeds Employment Programme for West Yorkshire Combined Authority. This 14-month project involved analysis of outcomes for 3,200 programme participants using difference-in-differences methodology, complemented by 45 depth interviews and 6 focus groups with beneficiaries and delivery partners. The evaluation found that the programme increased sustained employment rates by 12 percentage points relative to the comparison group, and our recommendations were adopted in full for the programme's second phase.

For this commission, we will structure the work in three stages: scoping and baseline establishment, ongoing monitoring and interim reporting, and final summative evaluation. We will use established evaluation frameworks and ensure that our approach is proportionate and ethical.

Our team includes experienced researchers and evaluators who understand the complexities of working in the public sector. We will ensure regular communication with the commissioning team and provide clear, accessible reporting that supports evidence-based decision making. All data collection will comply with GDPR and relevant ethical guidelines, and we will seek appropriate approvals before commencing fieldwork.

label: Excellent bid with detailed case studies, KPIs, risk mitigation, named roles
text:
Delivering the Community Mental Health Transformation Programme

Our Methodology

We will deliver this programme through four workstreams, each with a named clinical lead, operating across a 24-month delivery period. Our approach is informed by NICE guidelines NG216 and the Community Mental Health Framework for Adults and Older Adults (NHSE, 2019), and has been refined through two directly comparable programmes.

Workstream 1: Service Redesign (Months 1-6). Clinical Lead: Dr Hannah Wright, Consultant Clinical Psychologist (20 years NHS experience, formerly Clinical Director at South London and Maudsley). Dr Wright will conduct a comprehensive needs assessment using PHQ-9/GAD-7 population data, local JSNA findings, and service utilisation analysis. She led identical work for North East London ICB in 2024, where her redesign of the community pathway reduced average wait times from 47 days to 18 days and increased the proportion of patients receiving NICE-concordant treatment from 61% to 84%.

Workstream 2: Workforce Development (Months 3-18). Led by James Okonkwo, our Workforce Transformation Director (former Head of People, Cambridgeshire and Peterborough NHSFT). James will implement a competency framework and supervision model for 120+ staff. In his work with Sussex Partnership NHSFT, he designed a training programme that upskilled 89 community practitioners in trauma-informed care, achieving a 93% completion rate and a statistically significant improvement in staff confidence scores (pre: 3.2/7, post: 5.8/7, p<0.001).

Workstream 3: Digital and Data (Months 2-20). Technical Lead: Sana Malik, Senior Health Informatics Specialist. Sana will implement a real-time outcomes dashboard integrating clinical systems (Rio/SystmOne) with patient-reported outcomes. Our comparable deployment for Mersey Care NHSFT processes 14,000 clinical contacts per month and has been cited by the GIRFT programme as exemplary practice.

Workstream 4: Evaluation and Sustainability (Months 6-24). Research Lead: Dr Tom Fischer, Health Economist. Dr Fischer will conduct a prospective economic evaluation using a cost-consequence framework, reporting at 12 and 24 months.

KPIs and Targets
We commit to the following measurable outcomes: (1) 30% reduction in average waiting time for first clinical contact; (2) 20% increase in NICE-concordant treatment delivery; (3) 15% reduction in crisis presentations from the covered population; (4) patient experience score of 8.0+ on FFT; (5) staff retention rate above 85% across the programme period.

Risk Register
We have identified 14 risks rated amber or above. The three highest-rated are: staff recruitment in a competitive market (mitigated by our established bank of 40+ associate clinicians and a recruitment timeline that front-loads hiring to months 1-3); data integration delays (mitigated by early technical scoping in month 1 and a fallback manual reporting pathway); and stakeholder fatigue (mitigated by our community engagement lead, Fatima Al-Rashid, who will run a structured involvement programme with quarterly co-production events).

Delivery Timeline
Our detailed Gantt chart (Appendix B) maps all 47 deliverables across the 24-month period, with critical path dependencies highlighted and two months of float built into the digital workstream to absorb integration risks.

label: Over-promising bid with impressive language but no evidence
text:
Revolutionising Public Service Delivery Through Our World-Class Platform

We are uniquely positioned to deliver a transformative solution that will fundamentally reshape how the authority engages with citizens. Our award-winning platform, powered by state-of-the-art artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms, will deliver unprecedented levels of efficiency, accuracy, and user satisfaction.

Our solution will reduce processing times by up to 90%, increase citizen satisfaction scores to industry-leading levels, and generate savings of millions of pounds annually. We guarantee a return on investment within the first 12 months of deployment, with exponential benefits accruing over the contract period.

Our global team of over 500 experts spans every relevant discipline, from behavioural science to quantum computing, ensuring that we bring unmatched breadth and depth of expertise to this engagement. We have delivered similar transformations for governments on four continents, consistently exceeding expectations and redefining what is possible.

We will deploy our proprietary agile-at-scale methodology, which combines the best elements of SAFe, Spotify, and our own innovations to ensure rapid delivery without compromising quality. Our dedicated transformation squad will be on-site from day one, working shoulder-to-shoulder with your teams to co-create a bespoke solution that reflects the authority's unique context and ambitions.

This is more than a technology deployment. It is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to leapfrog into the future of public services.
